channel.title: The Daily Ledger
channel.description: Daily broadsheet covering markets, Bitcoin, geopolitics, and Chicagoland.

item.title: No. 36 — Warsh Makes His Global Debut in Sintra as Bitcoin Slides Below $58,500 to Cap Wall Street's Best Quarter Since 2020
item.description: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh joins Lagarde, Bailey, and Macklem at the ECB Forum in Sintra and declines to signal a July move, a day after the Dow's second straight record close (52,319.20) capped the S&P 500 and Nasdaq's best quarter since 2020; Bitcoin extends its slide to ~$58,300 after spot ETFs posted their worst month on record; Witkoff and Kushner report "positive" Doha talks with Qatar; Cubs and White Sox both look to sweep series finales amid an Extreme Heat Warning.

item.title: No. 35 — Stand-Down Holds, Doha Talks Open Today as Dow's Record Close Above 52,000 Sets the Tone for the Week
item.description: The U.S. and Iran agree to stand down after a weekend Hormuz exchange, with technical talks shifting to Doha Tuesday; Monday's Dow closed above 52,000 for the first time (+306.63, 7,440.43 S&P), lifted by Alphabet's Dow debut, Rocket Lab's $8B Iridium deal, and a Supreme Court ruling preserving Fed Governor Lisa Cook's seat; Bitcoin lags below $60K; Cubs beat Padres 3-2, White Sox rout Orioles 8-2; Chicago under an Extreme Heat Warning through Thursday.

item.title: No. 34 — Dow Closes Above 52,000 for First Time as Supreme Court Preserves Fed Governor Cook's Seat
item.description: The Dow closes above 52,000 for the first time ever as the Supreme Court's 5-4 ruling blocks Trump from firing Fed Governor Lisa Cook; Alphabet debuts in the Dow up nearly 5%; Rocket Lab announces $8B Iridium acquisition; Strategy authorizes its first-ever Bitcoin sell of up to $1.25B as BTC slides near $59,800; Cubs beat Padres 3-2, White Sox rout Orioles 8-2; Extreme heat warning issued for Chicagoland.

item.title: No. 33 — Iran Strikes Bahrain and Kuwait as US Resumes Attacks on Iranian Military Targets
item.description: Iran launches retaliatory strikes on Bahrain and Kuwait after the US resumes attacks on Iranian military targets, with Trump warning of further escalation; oil and markets digest the renewed hostilities ahead of the week's Doha-track diplomacy.

item.title: No. 32 — OpenAI Delays IPO to 2027 as SpaceX Collapses 32% Post-Debut and the Nasdaq Logs a Fifth Straight Losing Session
item.description: OpenAI pushes its IPO to 2027, SpaceX falls 32% from its debut high, and SoftBank drops 12.5% in a single session as the Nasdaq's losing streak extends to five days (week -4.6%); Bitcoin slides below $60,000 into Extreme Fear; White Sox rout Royals 22-1; Cubs drop the Milwaukee series opener 6-2; heat wave warning issued for the northwest suburbs.

item.title: No. 31 — Nasdaq and S&P 500 Tread Water Amid Tech Sell-Off, Reported OpenAI IPO Delay
item.description: Markets tread water as reports surface that OpenAI is delaying its IPO; tech sell-off continues to pressure the Nasdaq amid ongoing Iran ceasefire dynamics and Fed rate uncertainty.

item.title: No. 30 — Micron Delivers Its Strongest Quarter in History — $41.46B Revenue, $50B Q4 Guidance, and a 17% Pop Rescue the AI Rally
item.description: Micron's fiscal Q3 blowout — $41.46B revenue, $25.11 EPS, 84.9% gross margin — sends Nasdaq futures up 2% overnight and MU surging 17-18% on open; May PCE prints at 4.1% (3-year high) but in-line with estimates, analysts say May was the peak; Qualcomm +9%; Dansby Swanson sets Cubs franchise record with 11 RBI across a doubleheader sweep of the Mets (10-3, 10-5); Blackhawks trade #4 pick to Buffalo for Bowen Byram; NHL Draft Round 1 tonight in Buffalo.

item.title: No. 29 — All Eyes on Micron Tonight: Markets Attempt a Bounce After the AI Rout, But the HBM Number Will Decide Everything
item.description: S&P 500 futures edge higher after Tuesday's brutal session (S&P −1.44%, Nasdaq −2.21%) as markets await Micron's fiscal Q3 earnings at 4:30 PM ET; Bulls land Caleb Wilson #4 and Dailyn Swain #15; Giannis traded to Miami; Alphabet joins the Dow Monday; BofA calls for three Fed hikes in 2026.

item.title: No. 28 — The AI Gravity Check: SpaceX Slides, Korea Craters, and the Nasdaq Faces Its Worst Day Since the IPO Selloff Began
item.description: South Korea's KOSPI plunges 10%, Samsung and SK Hynix crater 12%+, SpaceX falls to $154.60 after its $20B bond sale, and Nasdaq futures drop 1.66% premarket; Bitcoin slips to $62,325; Oracle cuts 18,000 jobs citing AI; Micron earnings tomorrow; NBA Draft tonight with Bulls holding picks #4 and #15.

item.title: No. 27 — Starmer Out, Burnham In Line — The UK Gets Its 7th PM in a Decade as Bürgenstock Produces a 60-Day Roadmap
item.description: Keir Starmer resigns as UK PM on Monday morning as the Bürgenstock talks yield a formal 60-day Iran nuclear roadmap; Bitcoin pops to $64K; Intel surges 10.6% on Apple chip deal; NBA Draft at Barclays tomorrow with Bulls holding picks #4 and #15.

item.title: No. 26 — Vance Meets Iranians Face-to-Face at Bürgenstock — The 60-Day Nuclear Clock Is Ticking
item.description: JD Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner sit across from Iran's delegation at Switzerland's Bürgenstock resort on Sunday, opening the 60-day nuclear sprint under the Islamabad MOU.

item.title: No. 25 — Bürgenstock Talks Stumble Out of the Gate — Then Resume: The Iran MOU's First 48 Hours Are a Preview of What's Ahead
item.description: After Friday's abrupt postponement, US-Iran implementation talks resume Saturday at Bürgenstock with Witkoff and Kushner on-site.

item.title: No. 24 — Juneteenth 2026: Markets Pause, Bürgenstock Talks Begin, and Bitcoin Slides Into a Hawkish Long Weekend
item.description: NYSE, Nasdaq, and bond markets are dark for Juneteenth as Thursday's equity recovery rally (S&P +1.08% to 7,500.58) locks in a split verdict for the week.

item.title: No. 23 — Signed, Sealed, Delivered at Versailles: Trump and Pezeshkian Sign the Iran MOU as Markets Shrug Off Warsh's Hawkish Shock
item.description: Trump and Iran's Pezeshkian sign the war-ending memorandum at the Palace of Versailles, sending stocks sharply higher even as Wednesday's hawkish Fed dot plot rattled bond markets.

item.title: No. 22 — Decision Day: Warsh Delivers His First Rate Verdict at 2 PM as Bond Market Bets Shift From Cuts to Hikes
item.description: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC decision lands at 2 PM with a hold all but certain, but the dot plot and his debut press conference will show how a divided committee is leaning.

item.title: No. 21 — Kevin Warsh's Fed Era Begins: First FOMC Meeting Opens Today as Inflation Runs Hot and Trump Says He 'Loved' It
item.description: Kevin Warsh opens his first FOMC meeting as fed chair with a rate hold expected, May CPI at 4.2%, and Monday's Iran-deal rally still echoing through fresh record closes.

item.title: No. 20 — 'The Deal With Iran Is Now Complete': Trump Authorizes Reopening of Strait of Hormuz, Markets Surge to Start the Week
item.description: Trump declares the U.S.-Iran deal 'now complete' and authorizes reopening the Strait of Hormuz, sending Dow futures up 463 points.

item.title: No. 19 — Qatari Mediators Race to Tehran as Trump Declares Hormuz Deal 'Scheduled' for His Birthday
item.description: Qatari mediators race to Tehran for a potential breakthrough as Trump says a Hormuz-related deal is 'scheduled' to land on his birthday; S&P 500 rises 1.75% to 7,394.30.

item.title: No. 18 — 'Peace Has Never Been This Close': Pakistan Says U.S.-Iran Deal Text Finalized, Signing Could Come Within 24 Hours
item.description: Pakistan says a finalized U.S.-Iran deal text could be signed within 24 hours as the S&P 500 rises 0.5% to close just shy of 7,400.

item.title: No. 17 — SpaceX Roars Onto Nasdaq in Largest IPO Ever as Iran Deal Optimism Lifts Wall Street
item.description: SpaceX debuts on Nasdaq under ticker SPCX in the largest IPO in corporate history, raising roughly $75 billion, as Iran deal optimism lifts the Dow 354 points to 51,202.

item.title: No. 16 — Trump Cancels Iran Strikes — Markets Surge; Tornado Outbreak Hits Chicago
item.description: Trump cancels planned Iran strikes, sending the Dow up 930 points (+1.86%) and the S&P 500 up 1.75%, as a tornado outbreak hits northern Illinois and NW Indiana.

item.title: No. 15 — Dow Sheds 953 Points as CPI Hits Three-Year High — US Resumes Iran Strikes
item.description: Dow drops 953 points below the 50,000 floor and Nasdaq falls 1.98% as May CPI hits a three-year high of 4.2% and the US resumes strikes on Iran.

item.title: No. 14 — U.S. Launches Self-Defense Strikes on Iran as Markets Reel; Nasdaq, S&P Plunge on Tech Selloff
item.description: The U.S. launches self-defense strikes on Iran as the Nasdaq and S&P 500 fall roughly 1% amid a renewed tech selloff.

item.title: No. 13 — Chip Stocks Rebound; Nasdaq and Russell 2000 Book Small Comeback After Friday's Rout
item.description: Chip stocks rebound and S&P 500 futures rise 0.4% premarket as easing Iran hostilities help markets recover from Friday's 4%+ Nasdaq rout.

item.title: No. 12 — Week Ends on Sour Note: Nasdaq Falls 4.7% as Chip Selloff Accelerates on Hot Jobs Data
item.description: Nasdaq falls roughly 4.7% for the week as a hot May jobs report (172,000 vs. consensus) reignites fears the Fed will hold rates higher for longer.

item.title: No. 11 — Chips Crater — Nasdaq Suffers Worst Day Since April 2025 as Broadcom Disappoints
item.description: Nasdaq plunges 4.18% — its worst session since April 2025 — as Broadcom's guidance disappoints and chip stocks lead a broad selloff to S&P 7,383.74.

item.title: No. 10 — S&P Breaks 7,600 for First Time — Dow Hits All-Time High as Rotation Lifts Defensives
item.description: S&P 500 closes at a record 7,584.31, breaking 7,600 intraday, as defensive sectors lead a rotation-driven rally to fresh Dow highs.

item.title: No. 9 — Dow Smashes Record +1.73% as Chips Drag Nasdaq — Rotation Accelerates Into Jobs Friday
item.description: Dow Jones surges 874 points (+1.73%) to a fresh record as a rotation out of chip stocks accelerates ahead of Friday's jobs report.

item.title: No. 8 — Iran Drones Kuwait's Airport — Ceasefire Collapses, Oil Spikes, Stocks Fall
item.description: The four-day Iran ceasefire collapses after drone strikes on Kuwait International Airport; oil spikes to $97.80 and Bitcoin falls to $67,200.

item.title: No. 7 — Markets Reverse From Records — Bitcoin Plunges 31% in 9 Days as Saylor Breaks 'Never Sell' Pledge
item.description: S&P 500 falls 0.74% from its record as Bitcoin plunges to $72,400, down 31% in nine days, after Strategy's Michael Saylor breaks his 'never sell' pledge.

item.title: No. 6 — Iran MOU Signed — Markets Surge to New Records as Ceasefire Takes Effect
item.description: S&P 500 hits 7,621 and the Dow crosses 51,188 as a signed Iran memorandum of understanding sends markets to fresh records.

item.title: No. 5 — Month-End Recap: May 2026 Was One for the History Books — What June Holds
item.description: A look back at a record-setting May 2026 and a preview of what June has in store for markets, the Fed, and geopolitics.

item.title: No. 4 — May Ends at Records Across the Board — Dow Crosses 51,000 for the First Time
item.description: Dow Jones crosses 51,000 for the first time ever as May closes out with records across major indices.

item.title: No. 3 — S&P Hits Record Close as Iran Deal Framework Signals Ceasefire — PCE Inflation Complicates Fed Math
item.description: S&P 500 closes at record 7,609 as Iran ceasefire framework emerges from Oman talks; PCE inflation comes in hot at 3.8%.

item.title: No. 2 — Markets Shrug Off Iran Headlines — Dow Hits Second Consecutive Record Close
item.description: Dow notches second straight record close at 50,516 as Warsh confirmation odds rise and Iran ceasefire talks continue in Oman.

item.title: No. 1 — Dow Surges to Record as Chips Dump — Warsh Nomination Rattles Bond Market
item.description: Dow Jones gains 282 points to a record 50,234 even as chip stocks slump and the Warsh Fed nomination pushes the 10-year yield to 4.52%.
